programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police in the Great Plains are called about a dead body, a background check on the victim leads them into a bizarre web of homeless drifters, cattle auctions and bad checks — all fronted by elderly couple Ray and Faye Copeland.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1988, the body of a young woman was discovered in an Ohio river. Most of the evidence had been washed away, but the victim's 6-year-old son unwittingly told investigators all they needed to know — without saying a word. The clue was in his genes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, more than a dozen children in Seattle, Washington, were fighting for their lives. Each one of them had contracted a serious unknown illness. After one died, investigators knew the clock was ticking.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a successful stockbroker doesn't return home after a late shift, his wife reports him missing.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Soon after organizing a workers' strike, a union official is executed in his home. Neither his wife nor anyone in the neighborhood hear any gunshots. It takes a forensic sound test, an electron microscope and a nightgown to explain why.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: This examination of the role autopsies play in murder investigations focuses on the suspicious death of Virginia Ridley. Police charge her mentally unstable husband with murder, but a medical examiner discovers that she died of natural causes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The crime scene was especially violent: a husband and wife had been shot to death in their bedroom. Was their 16-year-old daughter complicit?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a Native American woman is brutally killed in the desert of New Mexico, her killers leave a crime scene rich in forensic evidence: tire tracks, shoe impressions and even the murder weapons.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1981, 6-year-old Cassie Hansen disappeared from her Minnesota church during an evening service. After receiving a tip that an older man was seen carrying a small person, police found Cassie's body in a dumpster.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Between 1985 and 1988, 18 people were choked, molested and left for dead in the remote mountains of California. The only witnesses were the insects — and they play an important role in solving the crimes and bringing the killer to justice.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a Philadelphia Convention, 180 Legionnaires contract pneumonia-like symptoms and 29 of them die. The determination of one scientist helps identify the cause of this deadly disease.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy real estate tycoon goes missing, it appears to be foul play. In his will, he leaves instructions in case he should die under violent circumstances — which were carried out after a hiker comes across his bullet-ridden skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is reported missing after a fight with her husband. Police suspect a man who reported a fire in his car. Two tiny drops of blood are found in the interior. Finally, a tiny clue inside the suspect's watchband helps solve the case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was found dead on the bedroom floor of her apartment. The crime scene yielded little of value and investigators wondered if they would find enough evidence to make a case, much less catch a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A three-year-old develops a respiratory condition. His father has Alzheimers. An investigator reveals that their ill health is due to mold in their modern home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Washington, DC is plagued by a series of arson fires — each started with the same type of incendiary device — it's up to forensic scientists to find the clues that lay in the ashes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Witnesses saw a woman in the back of a truck, flailing her arms and screaming. When they found a jacket near a pool of blood, they knew what they had seen wasn't a joy ride.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A twelve-year-old girl claimed she had been abducted and sexually assaulted. She recounted what happened in such a flat, unemotional voice that police found it difficult to believe her. Fibers on her clothing would prove she was telling the truth.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A college senior was found raped and murdered near an unpaved footpath used by students to walk from one side of campus to the other.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1986, Kathleen Lipscomb's body was found on a deserted road outside of San Antonio. Months and years passed, but the crime was never solved. Finally, a private investigator discovered Kathleen's diary.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The only clue recovered from a shooting is a twelve-guage shotgun. Scientists use unique methods to lift the serial number from the weapon to trace it to its owner

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a hit-and-run accident claimed the life of a high school athlete, everyone in town mourned his passing. Finding the killer was a long shot at best.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the decomposed body of a teenage girl is discovered, police have no clues to her identity. To solve the case, investigators turn to bug larvae found on the body and a tiny wad of chewing gum found near the victim's body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When an 11-year-old girl disappears from a remote Alaska town, investigators wonder if she has been attacked by a bear or become lost in the dense woods. Eventually, they realize that neither assumption is true.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, construction manager Darrell North was found dead in his work trailer in Ft. Worth, Texas. When investigators arrived, they found that he had been stabbed 46 times, indicating a personal relationship between the victim and the attacker.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: By using techniques she saw on forensic science TV shows, a mother determines who was responsible for the accident that permanently injured her 4-year-old daughter.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: It was the single most deadly automobile accident in American history. Almost a hundred vehicles were involved, twelve people died and more than fifty people were injured. And it happened along a stretch of highway long known for dense, thick fog.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a policeman's house goes up in flames, experts suspect arson. The officer whose home was torched arrests low-level drug dealer James Wren, who confesses. But when the suspect vanishes, investigators question the authenticity of the confession.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A brilliant young architect falls ill and dies just before she is to testify in a criminal trial. The autopsy reveals she'd been poisoned with arsenic. Investigators must determine who had a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of an American tourist in a Mexican resort draws law enforcement resources on both sides of the border. At first glance, the motive appears to be robbery, but careful analysis of the forensic evidence points to something more sinister.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Haunted by the disappearance of her mother, a young woman undertakes an investigation of her own. In her mother's diary, she discovers a dark family secret that might explain the disappearance.

programme.desc: Haunted by the disappearance of her mother, a young woman undertakes an investigation of her own. In her mother's diary, she discovers a dark family secret that might explain the disappearance.

programme.desc: When Leann Fletcher is found dead, investigators use blood spatter analysis and forensic computing to find a motive for her murder: her husband, a prominent lawyer, was having an affair with judge Susan Chrzanowski.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: A report on corruption and a cover-up by city officials of Tacoma, Washington, focusing on David Brame, the city’s police chief who rose to power despite a shady past.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: A report about how serial killer Andrew Urdiales was identified, arrested and convicted, and about the young woman who became the only one of his victims to survive.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: 17-year-old Sterling Barber was accused of murdering Gerald Douglas Wyatt, a church deacon. The Carnesville, Georgia teenager claimed that he acted in self-defense because Wyatt, known to be a predator, was trying to molest him.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: A report on families who have experienced the violent death of a loved one and believe they know the identity of the killer but lack evidence to back the charge.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: When Gertrude McCabe was murdered, her niece Jane Alexander was named her principal beneficiary. Jane, a widow in her 70s, began to suspect that her live-in boyfriend, Tom O'Donnell, might have been involved in the crime.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: A young banker named David Harmon was bludgeoned to death in his bed. Investigators came to believe that Melinda was involved in a secret affair with family friend Mark Mangelsdorf, and that the two were involved in David’s murder.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: 10-year-old Krystal Surles and her friend Kaylene Harris were attacked in Kaylene’s home by an intruder who slashed both girls’ throats. Kaylene died. Krystal not only survived but was able to help police, leading to the arrest of her attacker.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: For 17 years, Kim and Beth Jones have been trying to prove that their father, Jerry Jones, Jr. is innocent of the 1988 murder of their mother, Lee. Jones was found guilty, but in 1999, the court ordered him released due to an ineffective lawyer.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: DNA on one of two teenage girls killed in a similar fashion on a California beach six years apart leads investigators to a suspect who worked inside the police department.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: 48 Hours probes case of Alabama politician Stephen Nodine -- accused in the 2010 shooting death of his mistress, Angel Downs.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: A report on the 1997 murder of Doris Angleton, the wife of a successful Houston, Texas bookie. Doris’ husband Bob who claimed that his brother Roger was Doris’ killer, was tried for the crime in state court and acquitted. But the case did not end there.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Since 1969, more than 20 girls and women have disappeared or been killed along a road in, Canada, known as Highway 16.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: In November 2001, Michael Blagg returned home to find a bloody bed, but no sign of his wife Jennifer or their daughter Abby. When investigators learned that Blagg and his wife had argued over his pornography addiction, Blagg became the prime suspect.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: In June 2007, Dewayne Barrentine met Tausha Fields and soon after they began living together. Tausha told him some unbelievable stories about herself – leading Barrentine to verify her stories online, which resulted in the uncovering of a homicide.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.sub-title: Natalie Wood: Death in Dark Water
programme.desc: Los Angeles County Sheriff's investigators talk about the drowning death of actress Natalie Wood and suspicions about her then-husband, actor Robert Wagner.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: After a review of the circumstances surrounding the 1987 murder of Lita McClinton Sullivan and the police investigation of her husband, Jim Sullivan, the program follows Sullivan’s extradition from Thailand in 2004 and his ensuing trial for Lita’s murder.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: James Bernard Campbell entered Suezann Bosler's home, murdered her father and almost killed her. 48 HOURS follows Suezann as she returns to the crime scene and then focuses on her unusual crusade for justice.

programme.title: Deadliest Kids

programme.title: Deadliest Kids

programme.title: Holmes Inspection

programme.title: Holmes Inspection
programme.desc: Mike Holmes inspects a detached home with unusually high levels of humidity, and he suspects mold; Damon is on hand to fix all the issues found on Mike's inspection.

programme.title: Holmes Inspection

programme.title: Holmes: Buy It Right
programme.desc: Newlyweds Andie and Pete are ready to buy their first home in Englewood, Colo.; Mike steps in after the couple has toured over 100 houses, and he shows them when to walk away and when to make a solid bid.

programme.title: Holmes: Buy It Right
programme.desc: Newlyweds Jennifer and Matt choose houses in the Denver region, then they tour the structures with Mike who shows them how to tell whether a house is a good buy versus a house that deserves a goodbye.

programme.title: Holmes: Buy It Right
programme.desc: Mike Holmes travels to the island of Hilton Head, S.C., to advise first-time home buyers Caroline and Diego during their search; Mike goes through their favorite houses to identify issues that could be detrimental to the couple and their children.

programme.title: Holmes: Buy It Right

programme.title: Holmes: Buy It Right

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: To The South Pole: Rescue Truck arrives at Diego's Animal Center to deliver a cute, little animal that's really lost: a penguin! It's up to Dora, Boots and Diego to bring Pingüino all the way back home to the South Pole.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora Saves The Game: Dora's cousin Daisy is playing in a big soccer game that's showing on TV. But Daisy's team is short a player. Daisy appeals to Dora to come play on her team and save the game. Run, Dora, run!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: What Happens Next?: What comes after "The End"? Dora and Boots are finishing a fairy tale, and reach the end of the story. They wonder what happens next.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Rescue, Rescue, Rescue: Oh no! Three of our friends are in trouble. Baby Jaguar is stuck in a tree, Isa is stuck in a big sand hole, and Benny is stuck in his balloon on top of the Gooey Geyser. Who do we have to rescue first?

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Boot's Cuddly Dinosaur: It's bedtime, and Dora and Boots are having a sleepover. Dora has her teddy bear, Osito, but Boots can't find his cuddly dinosaur. Oh no! His dinosaur must have fallen out of his bag at Play Park.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: The Super Silly Fiesta: Bawk! It's fiesta time! Dora and Boots are invited to the Big Red Chicken's Super Silly Fiesta! There will be silly snacks, silly games, silly hats and even silly dancing.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: The Fix-It Machine: Uh oh! Boots has a hole - un hoyo - in his boot! But Dora knows what to do. They can go to the Super Duper Fix-It Machine to fix it right up!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Baseball Boots: Boots loves baseball. He loves to run and catch and throw, but so far he's never gotten a hit. Boots is determined to get his first hit. Dora and Boots have a big baseball game today.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Happy Best Friends Day! Today's a special day for best friends to play together. Boots and Dora decide to meet for a picnic to celebrate. Boots is bringing chocolate; Dora is bringing yummy strawberries.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: A is for armadillo, but where are the rest of the animals in the ABC book? They're missing! Dora and Boots jump into the giant alphabet book to help the little armadillo solve the mystery.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: What do you want to be when you grow up? On today's adventure, Dora and Boots get to try all sorts of jobs - from a pilot to a chef to an archeologist. But what will Dora decide to be when she grows up?

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Excited, Dora puts the Star Pocket on Backpack and Dora and Boots set off on their very first star catching adventure. Their educational journey will take them all the way to the Cloud Castle, as they try to rescue an adorable baby star.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora tells the story of how she first met Boots. On their very first adventure, Dora and Boots have to bring instruments back to the Fiesta Trio so they can play for the cranky Queen Bee.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Swiper swipes Dora's favorite necklace and flings it all the way to the top of Star Mountain! To get to the peak, Dora and Boots need to run up the Star Steps, climb up the Diamond, and make it through ice, wind, and snow!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: "Hola, hola!/ Hello, Hello!/ It's a great day for school/ You know!" Boots' music teacher, la maestra de música, needs to get to school in a hurry - but her bicycle is broken!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: It's Dora's cousin Daisy's 15th birthday party, her quinceañera! But the party can't start without Dora and Boots - they need to teach everybody the mambo!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora's cousin Diego protects and defends all the animals in the rainforest. But in order to save a Baby Condor, Diego winds up trapped himself. And now he needs OUR help! ¡No te preocupes, Diego!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Oh no! Baby Jaguar's best friend, Baby Bear, is stuck on a ledge atop Bear Mountain - and the ledge is cracking! With Baby Jaguar leading the way, Dora, Boots, and Diego race to the rescue!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Boots set out to help King Juan el Bobo find his crown so we can all go to his Mamí's birthday party. Where did the forgetful king put his crown?

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Abuela gives Dora and Boots a present for Dora's new puppy, but they'll have to watch out for Swiper, who's got a bunch of new swiping tricks up his sleeves.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora has some really exciting news to tell us: Someone new is joining Dora's family! Someone who sleeps in a cradle, drinks from a bottle, wears diapers and likes to be rocked to sleep. Can you guess who it is? That's right!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Oh, no! Dora left her music homework in Boots' room. It's a song in English and Spanish, and she needs Boots to bring it to her school so she can sing it to the class.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora's baby brother and sister just love bananas, but they also love stories! So Dora tells them a Super Babies story! The Super Babies have to get their bananas back from that Super Villain Swiper the Fox.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and all her friends are at the Super Adventure Race! To win the race, they have to make it across the Troll's tricky Bridge, past the Volcano filled with gooey balls, over the giant wall, and all the way to the finish line.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: It's summertime, and Dora and Boots are having a blast at the beach. But suddenly, they encounter the strangest sight - a snowman in Summer? What's wrong with this picture? Oh, no!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Boots are hiking on Rainbow Rock when they come across a shy rainbow, Arco Iris, hiding behind a waterfall.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Boots are having a blast at the beach when all of a sudden they hear a call for help. It's a baby crab stuck in a net!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Boots are playing catch when they discover a lost Baby Fox. And he wants his mommy! Just then, Swiper shows up to swipe Boots' superball.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Boots are playing catch when they discover a lost Baby Fox. And he wants his mommy! Just then, Swiper shows up to swipe Boots' superball.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora's twin brother and sister have been sick and she wants to buy them a present to cheer them up. Way up on a toy store shelf, she finds a dusty Jack-in-the-Box.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora's twin brother and sister have been sick and she wants to buy them a present to cheer them up. Way up on a toy store shelf, she finds a dusty Jack-in-the-Box.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: It's Twins Day and everyone's getting ready for the big Twins Day Party at Play Park! Dora even has a surprise for her puppy Perrito. He has a twin too -- a long-lost puppy brother he hasn't seen since they were babies.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: It's Twins Day and everyone's getting ready for the big Twins Day Party at Play Park! Dora even has a surprise for her puppy Perrito. He has a twin too -- a long-lost puppy brother he hasn't seen since they were babies.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: "Happy Three Kings Day!" Dora, Boots, and Diego are dressed up as kings for the big Three Kings Day party. They'll be bringing a horse, a camel, and an elephant to carry the presents, and treats for the party, but Swiper scares the animals away!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Isa are planting Isa's special unicorn flowers when suddenly a baby unicorn, Unicornio, rides in on a Rainbow. He's thrilled to see Isa's beautiful flowers because they're his Mami's favorite.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Benny is so excited – it's his first time in the Big Go-Cart Race, where everyone races in homemade go-carts! But when the whistle blows, Benny's cart backfires and falls to pieces. But Dora and Boots won't let him give up.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Benny is so excited – it's his first time in the Big Go-Cart Race, where everyone races in homemade go-carts! But when the whistle blows, Benny's cart backfires and falls to pieces. But Dora and Boots won't let him give up.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Boots and Tico are really excited because it's their first day of school ever -- and in their new school, they'll learn in English and Spanish!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Backpack, Backpack, yeah!!! Today's the Backpack Parade, where everyone dresses up their backpacks in different costumes. Backpack is super excited 'cause this year she gets to lead the parade with her song.

programme.desc: "Special delivery for Boots the monkey!" Boots' favorite boots are back from the Shoe Shop, but when he tries them on, he starts to bounce, bounce, bounce. These aren't his boots -- these are bouncy boots.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.sub-title: Dora Saves the Three Little Piggies
programme.desc: Dora and Boots are reading their favorite story, "The Three Little Pigs" when suddenly we hear the Piggies calling out for help. Their brick house is missing some bricks and the Big Bad Wolf is going to blow it down!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.sub-title: The Big Red Chicken's Magic Show
programme.desc: ABRACADABRA! The Big Red Chicken's putting on a magic show for all of his friends. With an "abracadabra," he pulls a rabbit out of his hat, but when it's time to make the bunny disappear, it doesn't work!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: It's Junk Day and Benny's showing Dora and Boots his wagon of cool junk. But when the junk truck accidentally takes Backpack and Map off to the junkyard, it's up to Dora, Boots, Benny and his wagon of stuff to rescue them!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: It's naptime for the twins and Dora has a special Super Babies naptime story. Once upon a time, the Super Babies were getting ready for their nap but the Dream Fairy never showed up because she was still asleep!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Pirate Piggy really wants to be in the big Pirate Treasure Hunt, but his Piggies got tummyaches. He needs at least 3 mates to help him find the sunken treasure. Dora, Boots, and L'il Piggy will be his pirate mates -- yay!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora reads a story about a Wizzle who could grant wishes with his magic wand. On his birthday, it's his turn to make a wish but the wand gets blown away by the wind. Dora needs your help to save the Wizzle's magic wand!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora reads a story about a Wizzle who could grant wishes with his magic wand. On his birthday, it's his turn to make a wish but the wand gets blown away by the wind. Dora needs your help to save the Wizzle's magic wand!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Boots are at an amusement park where they have a special wishing machine. Boots makes a wish: "I wish that when I say, 'bananas,' I get bananas." With a "boing," a bunch of bananas falls down on Boots.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Boots hop on Pegaso, the flying horse constellation, for a high-flying adventure to bring home his constellation friends to the Moon before the Sun rises!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Boots really wants to give his mommy a picture of himself for her birthday, but he's having a really bad hair day! Dora and Boots set out on a Hairy Quest: Boots is ready for his close-up!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Boots find a lovable little space creature named Winky. He came to visit with his family, our Purple Planet friends. But, uh oh, he's lost. We have to get Baby Winky back to his family before their spaceship takes off!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.sub-title: The Grumpy Old Troll Gets Married

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.desc: no info available

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: A Star of Dreams and Wishes: Taiki's Transformation
programme.desc: Ami invites Taiki to come and watch the Wataru’s Comet pass by Earth with their science teacher Amanogawa, only to be rejected.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: Luna's Discovery: The Real Face of Yaten
programme.desc: Usagi and the girls are searching for Luna, who has gone missing. To their surprise, they find Luna being introduced on TV as Yaten’s pet cat.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: Friend or Foe? Star Lights and the Sailor Guardians
programme.desc: Taiki asks Makoto to appear on a cooking show with him. Usagi decides to tag along, but her clumsiness creates a huge mess at the TV studio.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: Calling of the Shining Stars: Enter Haruka and Michiru
programme.desc: Usagi and the girls try to buy tickets to a Three Lights concert. Michiru gives them the tickets, revealing that she will be preforming.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.desc: Seiya asks Usagi out on a date, to which she agrees. Sailor Iron Mouse attempts to make up for her recent failures by going after Seiya’s star seed.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: Invaders from Outer Space: The Coming of Siren
programme.desc: Usagi and Luna encounter a girl who appears to be lost. The two are surprised to find the same girl now living in Usagi’s house as her younger sister.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: The Screaming Dead: Terror of the Camp Monster
programme.desc: Over the summer, the girls go camping in the mountains, where they run into the Three Lights, who are there shooting for an upcoming film.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: A Night Alone Together: Usagi in Danger
programme.desc: Usagi ends up agreeing to let Seiya spend the night at her place. Worried about leaving two teenagers together, the rest of the girls decide to join.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: Taiki's Song Filled with Passion and Faith
programme.desc: Taiki begins to question if their activity as Three Lights is really helping with their goal of finding their missing princess.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: Chibi-Chibi's Mystery: The Big Noisy Chase
programme.desc: Chibi-Chibi has been coming home with lots of snacks. Curious where she’s getting the snacks from, Usagi and the girls decide to follow Chibi-Chibi.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: The Shining Power of a Star: Chibi-Chibi's Transformation
programme.desc: Usagi teamed up with Seiya for the school softball tournament. Usagi becomes a target of Seiya’s jealous fans, who think she is hitting on him.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: Invitation to Terror: Usagi's Night Flight
programme.desc: Sailor Aluminum Siren learns that Usagi is Sailor Moon. She sets a trap on a plane in order to get Usagi’s attention.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: Duty or Friendship: Conflict Between the Sailor Guardians
programme.desc: A rift is created between the Sailor Guardians and the Star Lights after they discover each other’s secret identities.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: Truth Revealed: The Star Lights' Past
programme.desc: Uranus and the other Outer Guardians warn the Star Lights to never see Sailor Moon again. Usagi is worried about Seiya after he gets injured.

programme.title: Sailor Moon
programme.sub-title: Butterflies of Light: A New Chapter on the Horizon
programme.desc: Ami and the others try to clear up the misunderstanding the Three Lights have about Usagi.
